findings.title: Massive this.get() usage across codebase
findings.description: Found 6,779 occurrences of this.get() across 398 files. In Ember 3.28+ and Ember 4.x, this.get() on native classes is deprecated in favor of direct property access (this.propertyName). This is the single largest migration blocker.
findings.recommendation: Use the ember-no-get-codemod to batch-convert this.get('foo') to this.foo. Priority files: app-state.js (366 occurrences), persistence.js (76), user model (35+). Run: npx ember-no-get-codemod app/

findings.title: Massive this.set() usage across codebase
findings.description: Found 4,327 occurrences of this.set() across hundreds of files. In Ember 4.x, this.set() is removed in favor of @tracked properties with direct assignment. Must be addressed alongside component modernization.
findings.recommendation: Convert to @tracked properties with native setters. This requires converting classic components to Glimmer components first. Use ember-tracked-codemod for automated conversion.

findings.title: 2,572 {{action}} helper usages in templates
findings.description: The {{action}} helper is deprecated in Ember 3.28+ and removed in 4.x. Found 2,572 occurrences across templates. Must be replaced with @action decorator + {{on}} modifier or {{fn}} helper.
findings.recommendation: Replace {{action 'foo'}} with {{on 'click' this.foo}} and add @action decorator to the method. For components, use {{fn this.foo arg}} for argument passing.

findings.title: 100% classic components (215 components, 0 Glimmer)
findings.description: All 215 components use Component.extend() classic pattern. Zero Glimmer components found. Zero @tracked or @action decorators in components. The app is still entirely pre-Octane despite declaring 'octane' edition in package.json.
findings.recommendation: Begin incremental migration to Glimmer components starting with leaf components (components with no child components). Use the ember-component-codemod. Priority: simple UI components like confirm-*, badge-*, stats/* first.

findings.title: 1,375 computed() property usages
findings.description: Found 1,375 computed() property declarations across the codebase. These need to be replaced with @tracked properties and getters. Key hotspots: user/preferences controller (172), eval.js (128), sidebar-button-settings.js (97), badge.js (76), button.js (73).
findings.recommendation: Replace computed('dep1', 'dep2', function() {...}) with get propertyName() { return ...; } backed by @tracked dependencies. Convert in batches by module.

findings.title: 75 sendAction() calls still present
findings.description: sendAction was deprecated in Ember 3.4 and will be removed in 4.x. Found 75 occurrences across 21 files including critical components: button-listener.js (16), board-icon.js (5), subscription-form.js (8), grid-listener.js (6).
findings.recommendation: Replace this.sendAction('actionName') with calling the passed action directly: this.actionName() or this.args.actionName(). This is part of the closure actions migration.

findings.title: 37 files still use 'import Ember from ember' barrel import
findings.description: Found 37 files importing from the Ember global barrel import. This has been deprecated since Ember 3.x. All imports should use specific module paths like '@ember/object', '@ember/service', etc.
findings.recommendation: Run ember-modules-codemod to convert 'import Ember from ember' to specific module imports. Files include: app.js, i18n.js, all helper files, router.js, multiple services.

findings.title: 188 controllers still in use (heavy controller dependency)
findings.description: Found 188 controller files with 180 using Controller.extend(). Controllers are discouraged in Ember Octane in favor of component state. Many controllers contain complex computed property chains (e.g., user/preferences: 172 computed, sidebar-button-settings: 97, organization/reports: 36).
findings.recommendation: Gradually migrate controller logic into route-level components. Start with simple controllers that only pass data. Complex controllers like button-settings.js and user/preferences.js should be refactored last.

findings.title: All 210 component templates use separated template pattern
findings.description: Found 210 component templates in app/templates/components/ (old pattern) and 0 co-located templates in app/components/ (Octane pattern). Template-only components declared in package.json but not utilized due to classic Component.extend() pattern.
findings.recommendation: Move templates to co-located positions (next to their .js file in app/components/) as part of Octane migration. The ember-template-colocation-migrator tool can automate this.

findings.title: 102 lifecycle hook usages (didInsertElement, willDestroyElement, etc.)
findings.description: Found 102 occurrences of classic lifecycle hooks across 89 component files. Hooks like didInsertElement, willDestroyElement, didRender, willRender, and didReceiveAttrs must be replaced with Ember modifiers in Glimmer components.
findings.recommendation: Replace didInsertElement with {{did-insert}} modifier, willDestroyElement with {{will-destroy}}, and didReceiveAttrs with regular getters. Install ember-render-modifiers addon.

findings.title: Bower dependency on 9 packages including Bootstrap and jQuery plugins
findings.description: bower.json declares 9 runtime dependencies (moment ~2.9.0, tinycolor, jquery-minicolors, bootstrap ~3.3.2, recordrtc, IndexedDBShim, wordcloud2.js, hammer-time, qrcode-js). Bower has been deprecated since 2017 and is incompatible with modern Ember build pipelines.
findings.recommendation: Migrate all bower dependencies to npm packages. Replace app.import() calls in ember-cli-build.js with npm imports. moment -> luxon or date-fns, bootstrap 3 -> bootstrap 5 or tailwind, jquery-minicolors -> native color input.

findings.title: Asset fingerprinting and JS minification disabled
findings.description: ember-cli-build.js has fingerprint.enabled: false and minifyJS.enabled: false. This means production builds serve unfingerprinted, unminified assets, harming cache performance and increasing bundle size.
findings.recommendation: Enable fingerprinting and minification for production: fingerprint: { enabled: process.env.EMBER_ENV === 'production' }, remove the minifyJS override.

findings.title: Source maps disabled entirely
findings.description: ember-cli-build.js sets sourcemaps.enabled: false. This makes debugging production issues extremely difficult and prevents proper error tracking in tools like Sentry.
findings.recommendation: Enable source maps at least for development: sourcemaps: { enabled: process.env.EMBER_ENV !== 'production' }. Consider hidden source maps for production.

findings.title: Near-zero test coverage: only 3 test assertions in 102 test files
findings.description: Found 102 test files but only 3 actual test() calls (in 1 file). The only integration test is login-test.js. Zero unit tests, zero acceptance tests with actual test assertions. The test infrastructure exists but is essentially empty.
findings.recommendation: This is critical for an AAC app where regressions can block user communication. Prioritize: 1) Component rendering tests for board-canvas, button-listener, grid-listener. 2) Integration tests for board navigation flow. 3) Accessibility tests using ember-a11y-testing.

findings.title: jQuery integration disabled but 97 jQuery usages remain
findings.description: optional-features.json correctly sets jquery-integration: false, but the app still has @ember/jquery and jquery as devDependencies. Found 97 files importing or using jQuery patterns (this.$(), Ember.$, import jQuery). These are inconsistent with the opt-out.
findings.recommendation: Replace all jQuery DOM operations with native DOM APIs (document.querySelector, element.addEventListener). Remove @ember/jquery and jquery from package.json after migration.

findings.title: 48 transitionToRoute/replaceRoute calls in controllers
findings.description: Found 48 occurrences of transitionToRoute or replaceRoute across 32 files. These are deprecated in Ember 3.28 in favor of the RouterService (this.router.transitionTo / this.router.replaceWith).
findings.recommendation: Inject the router service and replace this.transitionToRoute('route') with this.router.transitionTo('route') and this.replaceRoute with this.router.replaceWith.

findings.title: ember-cli-eslint deprecated (replaced by eslint directly)
findings.description: The project uses ember-cli-eslint@5.1.0 which has been deprecated. Modern Ember projects run ESLint directly. Also, eslint@5.16.0 is very outdated (current is 9.x).
findings.recommendation: Remove ember-cli-eslint, upgrade eslint to v8+, install @ember/eslint-plugin, and configure eslint.config.js for flat config.

findings.title: ember-cli-template-lint outdated
findings.description: ember-cli-template-lint@2.0.2 is outdated. Modern Ember uses ember-template-lint directly (v5+) with updated rules for Octane patterns.
findings.recommendation: Replace ember-cli-template-lint with ember-template-lint@5+ and configure .template-lintrc.js with Octane-aware rules.

findings.title: eslint-plugin-ember@6.2.0 is severely outdated
findings.description: eslint-plugin-ember@6.2.0 is from 2019. Current is v12+. Missing all Octane-era lint rules like no-classic-components, no-computed-properties-in-native-classes, no-get, no-mixins, etc.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade to eslint-plugin-ember@12+ to get Octane migration lint rules that will catch deprecated patterns automatically.

findings.title: qunit-dom@0.8.4 severely outdated
findings.description: qunit-dom@0.8.4 is from 2018. Current is v3+. Missing modern assertion helpers that would improve test readability and AAC-critical accessibility assertions.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade to qunit-dom@3+ for improved assertions including hasAria(), hasAttribute(), isVisible() which are critical for AAC accessibility testing.

findings.title: Limited ARIA coverage: 322 aria/role attributes across 79 template files
findings.description: Found 322 aria-*/role= attributes across 79 of 447 template files (17.7% of templates). Given this is an AAC communication platform where users rely on assistive technology, ARIA coverage should approach 100% for interactive elements. Only 74 tabindex attributes found.
findings.recommendation: Audit all interactive templates for ARIA compliance. Key areas: board/index.hbs (communication board), button.hbs, button-listener, grid-listener. Add aria-labels to all buttons and interactive elements. Install ember-a11y-testing for automated checks.

findings.title: Limited keyboard/focus management (25 occurrences across 17 files)
findings.description: Found only 25 focus management patterns (focusIn, focusOut, focus(), keyDown, keyUp, keyPress) across 17 files. For AAC users who may use switch scanning, eye tracking, or keyboard-only access, comprehensive keyboard navigation is essential. scanner.js has 4 occurrences but needs more.
findings.recommendation: Implement comprehensive keyboard navigation for the communication board. Ensure all modal dialogs trap focus. Add keyboard shortcuts for common AAC actions. Test with screen readers (NVDA/JAWS/VoiceOver).

findings.title: 173 alt attribute usages but potential gaps in image accessibility
findings.description: Found 173 alt= attribute usages across 48 template files. While alt text is present in many places, an AAC app with symbol-based communication needs alt text on every image/symbol for screen reader users to understand button content.
findings.recommendation: Ensure every tag and symbol icon has meaningful alt text. For AAC board buttons, the alt text should match the button label. Use aria-label for non-image interactive elements.

findings.title: Custom i18n system (not ember-intl) with 2,212 i18n.t() calls and 7,646 {{t}} usages
findings.description: The app uses a custom i18n.js utility (2,175 lines) rather than the standard ember-intl addon. While functional, with 2,212 i18n.t() calls in JS and 7,646 {{t}} helper usages in templates, this custom system lacks pluralization rules, ICU message format, lazy loading, and locale fallback chains.
findings.recommendation: Consider migrating to ember-intl for proper ICU message format support, pluralization, and locale management. The custom i18n.t(key, default) pattern can be incrementally migrated.

findings.title: Hardcoded user-facing strings in templates
findings.description: Spot-check found hardcoded English strings in multiple template files (254+ occurrences of capitalized quoted strings in templates). While the {{t}} helper is widely used (7,646 calls), many templates still contain untranslated text.
findings.recommendation: Run a comprehensive scan for untranslated strings. Wrap all remaining hardcoded user-facing text in {{t 'key' 'default'}} calls. Focus on: button-settings-help.hbs (12), copy-board.hbs (38), brief.hbs (95).

findings.title: 1 Mixin usage found in application route
findings.description: Found Mixin reference in app/routes/application.js (commented out ApplicationRouteMixin). No active mixin files in app/mixins/ directory. This is positive - mixins are deprecated in Ember Octane.
findings.recommendation: No action needed - mixin has already been removed. Ensure no mixins are reintroduced.

findings.title: Babel polyfill included by default increasing bundle size
findings.description: ember-cli-build.js enables includePolyfill: true for ember-cli-babel. With modern browser targets (Node >= 18 requirement), most polyfills are unnecessary and increase bundle size significantly.
findings.recommendation: Set includePolyfill: false and configure targets.js to specify minimum browser versions. Use @babel/preset-env with useBuiltIns: 'usage' for targeted polyfilling.

findings.title: No code splitting or route-based lazy loading
findings.description: The ember-cli-build.js shows no code splitting configuration. All 216 components, 188 controllers, 447 templates, and bower vendor libraries are loaded upfront. For AAC users on slow connections or limited devices, this is a significant performance concern.
findings.recommendation: Implement route-based code splitting with ember-engines or embroider. Lazy-load stats components, organization admin pages, and setup wizards. This can reduce initial load by 40-60%.

findings.title: ember-welcome-page included in devDependencies
findings.description: ember-welcome-page@4.0.0 is a scaffolding artifact that shows a welcome screen when no index route is defined. While harmless as a devDependency, it adds unnecessary weight.
findings.recommendation: Remove ember-welcome-page from package.json.

findings.title: ember-maybe-import-regenerator@0.1.6 likely unnecessary
findings.description: This addon was needed for async/await support in older Ember versions. With ember-cli-babel@7.26.6 and modern targets, regenerator-runtime is included automatically when needed.
findings.recommendation: Remove ember-maybe-import-regenerator from package.json.

findings.title: Duplicate template structure (modals/ and components/ templates)
findings.description: Templates exist in both app/templates/ route-level and app/templates/components/ with many apparent duplicates (modals/valet-mode.hbs + components/valet-mode.hbs, etc.). This suggests a controller+modal pattern that should be refactored.
findings.recommendation: Consolidate modal templates. Use a single modal component pattern rather than controller+template pairs. Consider ember-modal-dialog or a custom modal service.

findings.title: Octane edition declared but not implemented
findings.description: package.json declares 'ember.edition: octane' and optional-features.json correctly sets template-only-glimmer-components: true and application-template-wrapper: false. However, the codebase is entirely pre-Octane. The edition flag enables Octane linting warnings but the code has not been migrated.
findings.recommendation: This is actually a positive setup - the edition flag and optional-features are correct for Octane. Now the code needs to catch up to the declared edition.

findings.title: Custom ESLint plugin (eslint-plugin-lingolinq) in use
findings.description: The project has a custom ESLint plugin at app/frontend/eslint-plugin-lingolinq/ suggesting project-specific linting rules are in place.
findings.recommendation: Ensure custom ESLint rules are updated to account for Octane patterns as the migration proceeds.

findings.title: Large utility layer with 32 utility files
findings.description: Found 32 utility files in app/utils/ including large files like i18n.js (2,175 lines), eval.js, edit_manager.js, button.js. These form a significant part of the app's logic layer outside the standard Ember patterns.
findings.recommendation: Consider refactoring large utilities into services for better testability and dependency injection. Priority: i18n.js -> i18n service, edit_manager.js -> editing service.

findings.title: ember-export-application-global still present
findings.description: ember-export-application-global@2.0.0 exports the app to the window global. This is a legacy pattern that interferes with tree-shaking and is removed in Ember 4.x.
findings.recommendation: Remove ember-export-application-global. If global access is needed for debugging, use Ember Inspector instead. Check for any code relying on window.LingoLinq and refactor.

findings.title: 107 files use Ember.run/runloop imports mixed with direct Ember import
findings.description: Found 107 files using Ember.run or importing from @ember/runloop. While the module import pattern is correct, many files mix both the barrel Ember import and specific module imports.
findings.recommendation: Consolidate all runloop usage to import from '@ember/runloop'. Replace Ember.run.later with import { later } from '@ember/runloop'.

migration_readiness.estimated_effort: 6-9 months for full Octane migration at current codebase size
recommended_migration_order: Phase 1: Tooling (1-2 weeks) - Upgrade ESLint, ember-template-lint, qunit-dom
Phase 2: Low-hanging fruit (2-4 weeks) - Barrel imports, transitionToRoute, Ember.run
Phase 3: Bower elimination (2-3 weeks) - Move to npm packages
Phase 4: this.get/set codemod (2-3 weeks) - Automated with manual review
Phase 5: Component migration (8-12 weeks) - Classic to Glimmer, batch by category
Phase 6: Controller reduction (4-6 weeks) - Move logic to components
Phase 7: {{action}} to {{on}} migration (3-4 weeks) - Template updates
Phase 8: Test coverage (ongoing) - Minimum 60% coverage target

score_breakdown.deprecation_compliance: 5/30 - 15,519 deprecated patterns, only optional-features configured correctly
score_breakdown.addon_compatibility: 10/15 - Ember 3.28 addons mostly compatible, but bower/eslint/qunit outdated
score_breakdown.testing: 1/15 - Near-zero test coverage, test infrastructure exists but empty
score_breakdown.accessibility: 4/10 - Some ARIA present but inadequate for AAC platform
score_breakdown.i18n: 6/10 - Custom system works, 75% coverage, but missing ember-intl features
score_breakdown.performance: 1/5 - No fingerprinting, no minification, no code splitting, polyfill bloat
